summary: buffer-util-extend impersonates feross/buffer and eval()s remote code at import time
details: On every require('buffer-util-extend'), a top-level IIFE in index.js base64-decodes the string "aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanNvbmtlZXBlci5jb20vYi9QVDBPTg==" to https://www.jsonkeeper.com/b/PT0ON, fetches the JSON body from this anonymous paste host, and passes data.content directly to eval(). The attacker can rotate the hosted payload at any time, so any installer that imports this package executes whatever code is currently served from that paste. The package additionally impersonates the popular `buffer` library: package.json copies Feross Aboukhadijeh's name, email (feross@feross.org), homepage (https://feross.org), and the github.com/feross/buffer repository URL verbatim, while shipping under the lookalike name buffer-util-extend. The combination of name/metadata impersonation, base64-obfuscated remote URL, anonymous mutable paste source, and direct eval of fetched content is an unambiguous supply-chain attack with arbitrary remote code execution on the installer's machine.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
